learning_outcomes.description: APST 1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of physical, social and intellectual development and characteristics of students and how these may affect learning.
APST 1.2 How students learn and implications for teaching
APST 1.3 Demonstrate knowledge of teaching strategies that are responsive to the learning strengths and needs of students from diverse linguistic, cultural, religious and socioeconomic backgrounds.

APST 1.4 Demonstrate broad knowledge and understanding of the impact of culture, cultural identity and linguistic background on the education of students from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander backgrounds.
APST 1.5 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of strategies for differentiating teaching to meet the specific learning needs of students across the full range of abilities
APST 2.2 Organise content into an effective learning and teaching sequence.
APST 2.4 Demonstrate broad knowledge of, understanding of and respect for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander histories, cultures and languages
APST 3.1 Set learning goals that provide achievable challenges for students of varying abilities and characteristics
APST 3.2 Plan lesson sequences using knowledge of student learning, content and effective teaching strategies
APST 3.3 Include a range of teaching strategies
APST 3.6 Demonstrate broad knowledge of strategies that can be used to evaluate teaching programs to improve student learning.
APST 4.1 Identify strategies to support inclusive student participation and engagement in classroom activities
APST 4.2 Demonstrate the capacity to organise classroom activities & provide clear directions
APST 4.3 Demonstrate knowledge of practical approaches to manage challenging behaviour.
APST 4.4 Describe strategies that support students wellbeing and safety working within school and/or system, curriculum and legislative requirements
APST 6.3 Seek & apply constructive feedback from supervisors & teachers to improve teaching practices.
APST 6.4 Demonstrate an understanding of the continued professional learning and the implications for improved student learning.
APST 7.1 Understand and apply the key principles described in codes of ethics and conduct for the teaching profession.
APST 7.2 Understand the relevant legislative, administrative and organisational policies and processes required for teachers according to school stage

assessments.assessment_title: Planning & Teaching Part A: Planning Adjustments
assessments.due_date:
12/08/2026 - 14/10/2026

Due 5:00pm, weekly on Wednesdays from Week 4 until Week 12.
assessments.task_description: Adjust your weekly planning for UniPE Primary in response to evidence and feedback from supervisors and critical friends.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: 5% of total mark is awarded when all lesson plans are complete and submitted on time.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: In small groups, produce a 12-minute podcast to examine an authentic story of challenging behaviour to analyse the strategies that teachers use to establish and maintain a safe and supportive learning environment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: One person from each group will upload your podcast to Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Planning & Teaching Part B: Teaching Showcase
assessments.due_date:
23/10/2026

On this date, each student will be scheduled for a 30-minute presentation and conversation with their Facilitator.
assessments.task_description:
Showcase how your planning and teaching led to productive learning opportunities for your students including the application of behaviour management frameworks and strategies. You will provide a narrative from your UniPE Primary Professional Experience and support this using primary evidence and academic literature.

In the event of disruption to the Showcase or prior data collection during UniPE, this assessment may be changed and/or the timing may be impacted.

Recording of Assessment:

Assessment Procedure

When a student is required to complete an oral or practical assessment item worth 20% or more, the performance will be recorded and retained as per Appendix 1 as the official recording of the item for re-mark purposes.

Recordings will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed for the purposes of:

i. moderation of marking

ii. provision of feedback to the student(s) recorded; and/or

iii. re-marking following a successful re-mark application.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
EDUC2004 Teaching Professional Experience includes Introduction Primary Professional Experience and UniPE Primary.

In order to receive a Pass grade for these professional experiences, you must:

a. complete Codes of Conduct and Student Protection Training relevant to your professional experience school sector;

b. be able to satisfy your Professional Experience supervisor that there is evidence of satisfactory achievement in your teaching performance; and

c. adopt the highest professional standards regarding personal presentation, interactions with school staff and students, attendance, punctuality and preparation.

An inability to meet these requirements will result in an automatic failure for these professional experience despite achievement in other criteria.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Annual certificate of sector-based Code of Conduct/Student Protection Training must be submitted to Blackboard before UniPE Primary begins.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction Primary Prac

1-week fulltime in a primary school working with an HPE teacher and in the classroom.
learning_activities.topic_description: Introduction Primary Prac 1-week fulltime in a primary school working with an HPE teacher and in the classroom.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 12(27 Jul - 18 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Planning & Reflection for UniPE Primary

In this series, you will engage in ongoing planning and reflection workshops designed to support your authentic needs during the UniPE Primary Professional Experience. You will reflect on, and refine your planning and teaching by (i) strengthening the alignment between learning goals, success criteria, teaching strategies, sequencing and scaffolding to enhance student progression; (ii) applying evidence-based classroom management practices to create positive, predictable and engaging learning environments.
learning_activities.topic_description: Planning & Reflection for UniPE Primary In this series, you will engage in ongoing planning and reflection workshops designed to support your authentic needs during the UniPE Primary Professional Experience. You will reflect on, and refine your planning and teaching by (i) strengthening the alignment between learning goals, success criteria, teaching strategies, sequencing and scaffolding to enhance student progression; (ii) applying evidence-based classroom management practices to create positive, predictable and engaging learning environments.

learning_activities.topic:
Teacher Presence

In this lecture, you will learn how teacher presence influences student behaviour through effective communication, positive relationships and clear expectations. You will explore strategies for creating safe, predictable learning environments and modelling respectful, calm, and consistent behaviour.
learning_activities.topic_description: Teacher Presence In this lecture, you will learn how teacher presence influences student behaviour through effective communication, positive relationships and clear expectations. You will explore strategies for creating safe, predictable learning environments and modelling respectful, calm, and consistent behaviour.

learning_activities.topic:
Managing Challenging Behaviour

You will examine the impact of effective pedagogical practices on student behaviour, engagement and learning outcomes. This will include establishing safe, predictable learning environments through positive teacher–student interactions and classroom management strategies such as clear expectations, rules and routines, attention signals, group formation, lesson transitions, use of space, and effective questioning and feedback techniques. These approaches support respectful, inclusive learning communities that maximise student engagement and learning. These practical skills will be rehearsed in class and applied throughout the UniPE Primary placement.
learning_activities.topic_description: Managing Challenging Behaviour You will examine the impact of effective pedagogical practices on student behaviour, engagement and learning outcomes. This will include establishing safe, predictable learning environments through positive teacher–student interactions and classroom management strategies such as clear expectations, rules and routines, attention signals, group formation, lesson transitions, use of space, and effective questioning and feedback techniques. These approaches support respectful, inclusive learning communities that maximise student engagement and learning. These practical skills will be rehearsed in class and applied throughout the UniPE Primary placement.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 4 To Week 12(10 Aug - 18 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
UniPE Primary professional experience

UniPE Primary allows you to develop your teaching skills, instructional strategies and professional practice while working with primary-aged students in a school setting. It enables you to apply the theories and strategies explored and rehearsed in EDUC2004 to establish and maintain safe, inclusive and learning-focused classroom environments. As a weekly placement experience, UniPE Primary offers ongoing opportunities to reflect on your teaching, refine your planning and continually enhance your professional practice.

Please note: There is no UniPE on student-free days or during school holidays.
learning_activities.topic_description: UniPE Primary professional experience UniPE Primary allows you to develop your teaching skills, instructional strategies and professional practice while working with primary-aged students in a school setting. It enables you to apply the theories and strategies explored and rehearsed in EDUC2004 to establish and maintain safe, inclusive and learning-focused classroom environments. As a weekly placement experience, UniPE Primary offers ongoing opportunities to reflect on your teaching, refine your planning and continually enhance your professional practice. Please note: There is no UniPE on student-free days or during school holidays.

learning_activities.topic:
Whole-School Behaviour Frameworks and Essential Skills for Classroom Management

You will be introduced to whole-school behaviour frameworks commonly used in schools, including Positive Behaviour for Learning (PBL). Using the Essential Skills for Classroom Management (ESCMs) as an example of a whole-school behaviour framework, you will learn how clear expectations, established rules and routines, and consistent responses to behaviour support student engagement and positive, supportive learning environments.
learning_activities.topic_title: Whole-School Behaviour Frameworks and Essential Skills for Classroom Management
learning_activities.topic_description: Whole-School Behaviour Frameworks and Essential Skills for Classroom Management You will be introduced to whole-school behaviour frameworks commonly used in schools, including Positive Behaviour for Learning (PBL). Using the Essential Skills for Classroom Management (ESCMs) as an example of a whole-school behaviour framework, you will learn how clear expectations, established rules and routines, and consistent responses to behaviour support student engagement and positive, supportive learning environments.

learning_activities.topic:
Behaviour & ALT-PE

In this lecture, you will explore how increasing students' time on task can improve behaviour management and learning outcomes. You will examine practical time-saving strategies such as routines, transitions and concise communication, to maximise active engagement in physical education lessons. Using the Academic Learning Time–Physical Education (ALT-PE) framework, you also will learn how to collect and analyse data on student and teacher time use. You will then apply these skills during your UniPE Primary placement to evaluate and enhance student engagement and learning opportunities.
learning_activities.topic_description: Behaviour & ALT-PE In this lecture, you will explore how increasing students' time on task can improve behaviour management and learning outcomes. You will examine practical time-saving strategies such as routines, transitions and concise communication, to maximise active engagement in physical education lessons. Using the Academic Learning Time–Physical Education (ALT-PE) framework, you also will learn how to collect and analyse data on student and teacher time use. You will then apply these skills during your UniPE Primary placement to evaluate and enhance student engagement and learning opportunities.

learning_activities.topic:
Emotional Intelligence & Difficult Conversations

In this lecture, you will explore how emotional intelligence influences your interactions with students, parents, and colleagues, and how self-awareness helps you respond calmly and proportionally. You will learn approaches for navigating difficult conversations professionally and constructively in order to negotiate conflict and strengthen relationships within the school community.
learning_activities.topic_description: Emotional Intelligence & Difficult Conversations In this lecture, you will explore how emotional intelligence influences your interactions with students, parents, and colleagues, and how self-awareness helps you respond calmly and proportionally. You will learn approaches for navigating difficult conversations professionally and constructively in order to negotiate conflict and strengthen relationships within the school community.

learning_activities.topic:
Crisis Management

Through complex behavioural scenarios, you will develop the knowledge and skills needed to respond effectively to challenging behaviour in crisis situations. Drawing on principles from Teacher Presence and Emotional Intelligence lectures, and relevant school policies and procedures, you will practise maintaining a calm, confident, and professional approach. Emphasis is placed on prioritising safety, de-escalating situations, and responding proportionally to student behaviour.
learning_activities.topic_description: Crisis Management Through complex behavioural scenarios, you will develop the knowledge and skills needed to respond effectively to challenging behaviour in crisis situations. Drawing on principles from Teacher Presence and Emotional Intelligence lectures, and relevant school policies and procedures, you will practise maintaining a calm, confident, and professional approach. Emphasis is placed on prioritising safety, de-escalating situations, and responding proportionally to student behaviour.

learning_activities.topic:
Questioning & Feedback

In this lecture, you will explore the fundamentals of effective questioning and feedback as powerful tools for enhancing student learning outcomes. You will develop supportive strategies for using questioning and feedback to guide student progress, promote higher-order thinking and inform teaching decisions. These strategies will be applied in UniPE Primary placement.
learning_activities.topic_description: Questioning & Feedback In this lecture, you will explore the fundamentals of effective questioning and feedback as powerful tools for enhancing student learning outcomes. You will develop supportive strategies for using questioning and feedback to guide student progress, promote higher-order thinking and inform teaching decisions. These strategies will be applied in UniPE Primary placement.

learning_activities.topic:
Diverse Classrooms: Inclusion & Differentiation

In this lecture you will explore how Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI) principles and Universal Design for Learning (UDL) support the creation of safe, predictable and respectful learning environments where all students, particularly those from diverse linguistic, cultural, religious, socioeconomic and ability backgrounds, can participate and succeed. You will learn how strengths-based, inclusive practices and appropriate adaptations can reduce barriers to learning, maximise participation, uphold social justice, and meet the diverse needs of all learners.
learning_activities.topic_description: Diverse Classrooms: Inclusion & Differentiation In this lecture you will explore how Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI) principles and Universal Design for Learning (UDL) support the creation of safe, predictable and respectful learning environments where all students, particularly those from diverse linguistic, cultural, religious, socioeconomic and ability backgrounds, can participate and succeed. You will learn how strengths-based, inclusive practices and appropriate adaptations can reduce barriers to learning, maximise participation, uphold social justice, and meet the diverse needs of all learners.

learning_activities.topic:
Diverse Classrooms: Trauma Informed Pedagogies

In this lecture, you will explore the impact of trauma on young people’s learning, wellbeing and behaviour, and examine why trauma-aware pedagogies are essential in diverse classrooms. You will develop an understanding of how trauma can affect brain functioning, relationships and participation, while recognising students’ strengths, resilience and capacity to thrive in supportive environments. You will also learn practical strategies for creating trauma-informed learning environments by building relationships and community, supporting social and emotional learning, and using responsive pedagogies that foster safety, belonging and inclusion for all students.
learning_activities.topic_description: Diverse Classrooms: Trauma Informed Pedagogies In this lecture, you will explore the impact of trauma on young people’s learning, wellbeing and behaviour, and examine why trauma-aware pedagogies are essential in diverse classrooms. You will develop an understanding of how trauma can affect brain functioning, relationships and participation, while recognising students’ strengths, resilience and capacity to thrive in supportive environments. You will also learn practical strategies for creating trauma-informed learning environments by building relationships and community, supporting social and emotional learning, and using responsive pedagogies that foster safety, belonging and inclusion for all students.

learning_activities.topic:
Diverse Classrooms: Gifted & Talented

In this lecture, you will explore the characteristics and needs of gifted and talented learners. You will learn broad strategies for differentiation to support these learners by creating a safe learn environments that supports their social, emotional and academic development.
learning_activities.topic_description: Diverse Classrooms: Gifted & Talented In this lecture, you will explore the characteristics and needs of gifted and talented learners. You will learn broad strategies for differentiation to support these learners by creating a safe learn environments that supports their social, emotional and academic development.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 12 To Week 13(12 Oct - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Preparation for Teaching Showcase assessment

In preparation for your Teaching Showcase assessment, this series will support you to analyse significant planning and teaching decisions from your UniPE Primary experience. Through this process, you will develop a professional narrative that draws on evidence collected during your teaching to explain how you identified, responded to, and reflected on key learning and teaching issues. You will also consider the impact of these decisions on student learning and how the insights gained will inform your future teaching practice.
learning_activities.topic_description: Preparation for Teaching Showcase assessment In preparation for your Teaching Showcase assessment, this series will support you to analyse significant planning and teaching decisions from your UniPE Primary experience. Through this process, you will develop a professional narrative that draws on evidence collected during your teaching to explain how you identified, responded to, and reflected on key learning and teaching issues. You will also consider the impact of these decisions on student learning and how the insights gained will inform your future teaching practice.

learning_activities.topic:
Diverse Classrooms: Culturally Responsive Pedagogy

In this lecture, you will explore strengths-based, responsive pedagogies for creating culturally safe, inclusive learning environments for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. You will also learn strategies for working respectfully with families and communities to reduce barriers to learning, maximise participation and support educational outcomes.
learning_activities.topic_description: Diverse Classrooms: Culturally Responsive Pedagogy In this lecture, you will explore strengths-based, responsive pedagogies for creating culturally safe, inclusive learning environments for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. You will also learn strategies for working respectfully with families and communities to reduce barriers to learning, maximise participation and support educational outcomes.
